stories.headline: Iran war live: US kills 2, wounds 11; Houthis attack 2 Saudi oil tankers
stories.summary: US and Iran trade attacks as more explosions are reported in Iranian cities, including Sirik, Ahvaz and Bushehr.

stories.headline: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US Department of Energy says the "peaceful" co-operation agreement will give US firms "great access" to the Saudi nuclear energy programme.

stories.headline: Saudi Arabia and US agree to landmark nuclear deal for civilian purposes
stories.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia have agreed to a civilian nuclear deal allowing Riyadh to build nuclear reactors.

stories.headline: US announces landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The Trump administration did not release details of the deal, but the framework could potentially provide Saudi Arabia with a facility that would allow it to enrich uranium.

stories.headline: Houthis claim attack on oil tankers as US launches more strikes on Iran
stories.summary: The Houthi attacks would be the first in the Red Sea since the Iran-backed group announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia.

stories.headline: Iran-backed Houthis attack two Saudi Arabian oil tankers
stories.summary: Brent prices rise after Yemeni rebels say they hit the Encelia and the Layla

stories.headline: Israel building earthen barrier in Gaza to entrench division of the enclave
stories.summary: The construction of the barrier has fuelled Palestinian fears that most of Gaza will never be relinquished by Israel.

stories.headline: AusAlert test date and time: emergency warning siren will blast over most phones in Australia on Monday. Here’s what to know
stories.summary: National emergency test alert will be sent to all mobile devices on 27 July, with domestic violence advocates advising how to turn off safe phonesFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastVirtually every phone in the country will blare an emergency siren at 2pm AEST on Monday 27 July as part of the first Australia-wide test of a new national warning system called AusAlert.Most

stories.headline: Europe Today: Latvian PM Andris Kulbergs speaks exclusively to Euronews as Rubio meets Lavrov
stories.summary: Andris Kulbergs became Latvia's Prime Minister in May after the previous coalition government collapsed over its handling of stray drones. He speaks exclusively to Euronews as US Secretary of State, Marco Rubio, meets today Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov to discuss ending the war in Ukraine.

stories.headline: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.

stories.headline: US oil refineries run at breakneck speeds as wars choke fuel supplies
stories.summary: High utilisation rates create risks of outages at a time when fuel prices are already up sharply

stories.headline: China Curbs Loan Structure Widely Used by Distressed Developers
stories.summary: China is restricting real estate companies from using a popular offshore loan structure that has helped some of them weather the country’s property crisis, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to recent moves to strengthen oversight of overseas borrowing.

stories.headline: Amish Shah beats Democratic establishment pick in House primary in Arizona
stories.summary: Rep. Andy Biggs, who voted to overturn the 2020 election results, won the GOP nomination for governor. Among Democrats, a left-wing challenger failed to unseat Rep. Greg Stanton.

stories.headline: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
stories.summary: Canada's dairy supply management system is in the spotlight after the US president singled it out as one of three main irritants used to justify new tariffs.

stories.headline: Blocked by censors, China's animal lovers take fight against abuse offline and overseas
stories.summary: The killing of a dog and her puppies in Guangdong has sparked outrage inside and outside the country.

stories.headline: Japan awakes
stories.summary: Why 1 per cent interest rates could shake everything up after a generation of deflation

stories.headline: Trump Media fast feed for president’s posts sparks Wall Street backlash
stories.summary: New venture has raised questions as financial firms decipher risk of paying for advanced access to market-moving feed

stories.headline: Hong Kong, Malaysia Collaborate to Broaden Cross-Market Access
stories.summary: Hong Kong and Malaysia will work together to expand access to capital and deepen cross-border participation in both markets as they look to attract more international investment.

stories.headline: Roche’s Second-Quarter Sales Held Back by Blockbuster Eye Drug
stories.summary: Roche Holding’s results showed a slower-than-expected recovery for eye blockbuster Vabysmo as investors look to a key new drug to power growth ahead.

stories.headline: China Bond Rally Shows Weak Economic Sentiment Before Politburo
stories.summary: China’s sovereign bonds are rallying once again as economic weakness, sliding stocks and expectations for greater monetary policy support revive demand for fixed-income assets.

stories.headline: India’s Top Asset Manager Said to Plan $200 Million Tech Fund
stories.summary: SBI Funds Management is planning its first technology-focused fund for private firms, people familiar with the matter said, as India’s largest asset manager follows rivals in bulking up in the fast-growing alternative investment fund industry.

stories.headline: BNP Paribas Stock Traders Ride Wall Street Rally With 43% Gain
stories.summary: BNP Paribas SA reported better-than-expected revenue and profit for the second quarter as its stock traders blew past analysts’ estimates.

stories.headline: Indonesian Stocks Rise 20% From Low in June, Set for Bull Market
stories.summary: A rally in Indonesian stocks put the benchmark index on track for a bull market, helped by an investor rotation into laggards and an easing of concerns about the country’s fiscal health.

stories.headline: China’s Semight Is Said to Mull Hong Kong Listing After 2,200% Stock Rise
stories.summary: Semight Instruments Co. is considering a second listing in Hong Kong, people familiar with the matter said, after shares of the Chinese maker of test instruments for semiconductors surged roughly 2,200% since their debut in April.

stories.headline: China Solar Lobby Says Fewer Installs Mark Sustainable Shift
stories.summary: China’s solar lobby said the industry is on a more sustainable track, after a policy shift drove a 66% decline in installations in the first half of 2026.

stories.headline: Australian Employment Soars in June, Bolstering Rate-Hike Bets
stories.summary: Australian employment surged in June, extending the previous month’s gains, underscoring the ongoing strength in the nation’s labor market and stoking bets on another interest-rate increase.

stories.headline: Fund Managers Want Taiwan Regulators to Lift a Mobile Phone Ban
stories.summary: When fund managers in Taiwan arrive at work each morning, they have to lock away their mobile phones before the stock and bond markets open, and can’t retrieve their devices until the end of the trading day.

stories.headline: Republican Lawmaker Urges Charges Against Jack Smith
stories.summary: The criminal referral could provide a catalyst for a prosecution of Mr. Smith, who as special counsel became a top target of the president’s retribution campaign.

stories.headline: Indian police accused of using pellet guns in crackdown on India’s protests
stories.summary: Videos emerge showing police using pellet guns on protesters India’s student protest movement.

stories.headline: Calls for Moroccan rapper’s release outside a Casablanca court
stories.summary: Protesters gathered outside a court in Casablanca, Morocco to demand the release of Moroccan rapper Mehdi El Youbi.

stories.headline: British woman jailed for blackmail after accusing banker of rape in Hong Kong
stories.summary: Isabel Rose was convicted of trying to extort £100,000 from the UK banker and perverting the course of justice.

stories.headline: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs

stories.headline: Massachusetts House Passes Bill That Would Loosen Late-Term Abortion Rules
stories.summary: The bill would leave it to physicians to decide if an abortion is necessary after 24 weeks of pregnancy. Current law allows such abortions only under certain circumstances.

stories.headline: Rubio and Chinese Diplomat Make Plans for Trump and Xi’s Washington Summit
stories.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spent much of a meeting with China’s foreign minister discussing the summit, but did not bring up President Trump’s claims of election interference by China.

stories.headline: British woman jailed in Hong Kong for blackmail after accusing banker of rape
stories.summary: Isabel Rose, 26, sentenced to six years for blackmail and perverting course of public justice over her allegations against British manA British woman who reported being raped to police in Hong Kong has been sentenced to six years in prison for blackmail and perverting the course of public justice.Isabel Rose, 26, from south-east London, travelled to Hong Kong in early 2024 to visit a British banker she had met while travelling in south-east Asia.

stories.headline: EU gives conditional green light to Paramount's Warner Bros. Discovery deal
stories.summary: Paramount's bid still faces hurdles across the Atlantic after a federal judge in California ordered the companies to temporarily pause their merger this week.

stories.headline: Hungary's prosecutor resigns under pressure in political win for Magyar
stories.summary: Hungary's Prosecutor General, Gábor Bálint Nagy, has resigned amid mounting political pressure as Prime Minister Péter Magyar seeks to replace Orbán-era officials. The move follows allegations of political bias by lawyers for detained Ukrainian cash couriers in a cross-border cash seizure case.

stories.headline: A year after deadly jet crash at Bangladesh school, families demand answers
stories.summary: This week marks one year since a military jet struck a school in Dhaka, killing 36, most of them children.

stories.headline: Why are thousands protesting for education reform in India?

stories.headline: Asian Shares Rise on Chipmakers, Brent Tops $96: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Asian shares rose as regional chipmakers climbed on expectations they will benefit from billions of dollars flowing into the artificial intelligence buildout. Oil extended its recent rally.

stories.summary: Michael Kratsios, President Trump’s science adviser, proposed overhauling how the government funds research. Democrats said Mr. Trump’s actions had weakened science.

stories.summary: The Tokyo Metropolitan Government wants workers to ditch the suit and tie for t-shirts, trainers - and shorts.

stories.summary: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.

stories.headline: Moment fans in Madrid celebrate World Cup win
stories.summary: Thousands of spectators watched Spain's victory in central Madrid on late Sunday night.

stories.headline: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
stories.summary: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

stories.headline: How these twins' rare growth disorder could provide the key to preventing cancer

stories.headline: US strikes oil tanker with missiles as it enforces new Iran blockade
stories.summary: The US military targeted an unladen oil tanker which it said was heading for the key Iranian oil terminal on Kharg island.

stories.headline: US launches fresh strikes on Iran as Trump warns Tehran it 'better behave'
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says he is yet to decide whether or not he will "finish off" Iran.
